symptoms.symptom_name: Acute choking/coughing on a fluid feed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A witnessed choking event on fluid in a child with bulbar dysphagia is the trigger for aspiration and airway soiling.
Onset at the moment of a feed raises acute mechanical obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transient perioral cyanosis during the episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cyanosis indicates significant transient hypoxaemia from airway compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ongoing wet inspiratory stridor with intercostal recession

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Wet inspiratory stridor with recession localises the problem to the upper airway and shows continuing compromise.
The stridor is wet and feed-related rather than a dry barking cough on a viral background.
Borderline hypoxaemia supports significant compromise; a clinician-measured value not obtainable via chat without a validated device.
Sympathetic and respiratory-drive response to hypoxaemia and distress; measured clinician vitals, sealed and not reliably obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transmitted upper-airway rhonchi with clear lung bases on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear bases with transmitted upper-airway sounds point to pooled secretions rather than consolidation; requires a stethoscope, so sealed.
Absence of focal crackles argues against established consolidation at this moment, though delayed pneumonia remains a risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cranial-nerve deficit affecting gag reflex and palatal elevation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Impaired gag and palatal elevation is the mechanistic cause of the aspiration; established on neurological examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Healed sternotomy scar with grade 2/6 systolic murmur

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Repaired congenital heart disease (incidental to the acute event)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Known baseline cardiac history; not the driver of the acute airway problem but relevant background.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no preceding viral illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/prodrome argues against an infective cause at onset, favouring a mechanical aspiration event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known severe swallowing difficulty with a complex repaired airway (choanal atresia, tracheo-oesophageal fistula)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Baseline dysphagia and complex airway anatomy substantially raise both the likelihood and the danger of aspiration events.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound deafness and non-verbal status (cannot self-report distress)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to self-report severity is why the threshold for in-person review must be lowered and reasoning must rely on carer-observable signs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Alert, not drowsy or difficult to wake at present

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring now, but a normal conscious level does not exclude rapid decompensation and must be re-checked continuously.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stridor relieved by oropharyngeal suctioning of pooled secretions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Response of the stridor to suctioning confirms pooled secretions as the mechanism; suctioning is an in-person intervention unavailable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute micro-aspiration with impending airway compromise
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Choking on fluid, transient cyanosis and ongoing wet stridor in a child with baseline bulbar dysphagia together define a high-risk aspiration/airway emergency requiring emergency in-person care.
A repaired complex airway plus inability to self-report severity both lower the safe threshold for escalation and raise the danger of any given event.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person airway assessment with continuous pulse oximetry and paramedic/ENT review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A complex-airway child with ongoing stridor, recent cyanosis and borderline saturations needs urgent professional airway assessment, suctioning and oxygen — none available via chat.
To assess for aspiration pneumonitis/pneumonia if fever, wet cough or worsening respiratory signs develop over the next 24-48h; decided in person, not reflexively.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Videofluoroscopic / instrumental swallow assessment by speech pathology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To reassess fluid consistency and swallow safety after the acute event is stabilised; a scheduled specialist investigation, not an acute telehealth action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine blood tests in the acute setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not a telehealth action and not the priority — bloods must not delay recognition and escalation of the airway problem.
